ddls:
CREATE TABLE "mice" (
  "mouse_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mouse */,
  "mouse_name" TEXT /* Name of the mouse */,
  "species" TEXT /* Species of the mouse */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the mouse */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Date of birth of the mouse */,
  "strain" TEXT /* Strain of the mouse */,
  "breed" TEXT /* Breed of the mouse */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight of the mouse in grams */,
  "genotype" TEXT /* Genotype of the mouse */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mouse_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "imaging_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each imaging session */,
  "mouse_id" INTEGER /* ID of the mouse involved in the session */,
  "session_date" TEXT /* Date of the imaging session */,
  "session_time" TEXT /* Time of the imaging session */,
  "imaging_protocol" INTEGER /* ID of the imaging protocol used */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the imaging data file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the imaging data file in MB */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the imaging data file */,
  "equipment_used" TEXT /* Equipment used for imaging */,
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operator who performed the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_imaging_sessions_mouse_id FOREIGN KEY ("mouse_id") REFERENCES mice ("mouse_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_imaging_sessions_operator_id FOREIGN KEY ("operator_id") REFERENCES operators ("operator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_imaging_sessions_imaging_protocol FOREIGN KEY ("imaging_protocol") REFERENCES protocols ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the imaging session */,
  "LVIDd_auto" REAL /* Left ventricular internal diameter during diastole (LVIDd) measured automatically */,
  "LVIDd_auto_time" REAL /* Time at which LVIDd was measured automatically */,
  "LVIDs_auto" REAL /* Left ventricular internal diameter during systole (LVIDs) measured automatically */,
  "LVIDs_auto_time" REAL /* Time at which LVIDs was measured automatically */,
  "heart_rate_auto" REAL /* Heart rate measured automatically */,
  "heart_rate_auto_time" REAL /* Time at which heart rate was measured automatically */,
  "LVIDd_manual" REAL /* Left ventricular internal diameter during diastole (LVIDd) measured manually */,
  "LVIDd_manual_time" REAL /* Time at which LVIDd was measured manually */,
  "LVIDs_manual" REAL /* Left ventricular internal diameter during systole (LVIDs) measured manually */,
  "LVIDs_manual_time" REAL /* Time at which LVIDs was measured manually */,
  "heart_rate_manual" REAL /* Heart rate measured manually */,
  "heart_rate_manual_time" REAL /* Time at which heart rate was measured manually */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_measurements_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES imaging_sessions ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quality_labels" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality label */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the measurement */,
  "LVIDd_auto_quality_label" REAL /* Quality label for LVIDd measurement (automated) */,
  "LVIDd_auto_quality_flag" TEXT /* Quality flag for LVIDd measurement (automated) */,
  "LVIDs_auto_quality_label" REAL /* Quality label for LVIDs measurement (automated) */,
  "LVIDs_auto_quality_flag" TEXT /* Quality flag for LVIDs measurement (automated) */,
  "heart_rate_auto_quality_label" REAL /* Quality label for heart rate measurement (automated) */,
  "heart_rate_auto_quality_flag" TEXT /* Quality flag for heart rate measurement (automated) */,
  "LVIDd_manual_quality_label" REAL /* Quality label for LVIDd measurement (manual) */,
  "LVIDd_manual_quality_flag" TEXT /* Quality flag for LVIDd measurement (manual) */,
  "LVIDs_manual_quality_label" REAL /* Quality label for LVIDs measurement (manual) */,
  "LVIDs_manual_quality_flag" TEXT /* Quality flag for LVIDs measurement (manual) */,
  "heart_rate_manual_quality_label" REAL /* Quality label for heart rate measurement (manual) */,
  "heart_rate_manual_quality_flag" TEXT /* Quality flag for heart rate measurement (manual) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_labels_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES measurements ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the imaging session being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the session */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the session was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_duration" REAL /* Duration of access in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES imaging_sessions ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the imaging protocol */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the imaging protocol */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the protocol */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the protocol */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the protocol was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_protocols_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operators" (
  "operator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operator */,
  "operator_name" TEXT /* Full name of the operator */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the operator */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution of the operator */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the operator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operator_id")
)
